customer_anxiety_profile: Sanobio is a low-intent, cost-skeptical customer who filled out a form expecting a ballpark estimate or access to a free state solar program — not a sales call. He has a call-screening service that blocks unknown numbers, explicitly refused a credit pull, and booked with 'not right now' still on the table. His primary anxiety is pricing opacity: he suspects Ion will price just below his current bill and capture the margin. He is not hostile, but he is not convinced.
unresolved_concerns: Whether Ion's program is materially better than waiting for the Illinois state free solar program
Pricing transparency — specifically, whether Ion's rate will produce real savings or just a 'penny lower' than ComEd
Per-kWh rate (asked directly, deferred to specialist — closer must address this early)
Credit pull — Sanobio explicitly refused; closer should not lead with credit qualification
sensitive_topics_raised: Illinois state free solar program — Sanobio believes this exists and was waiting for it; closer should be prepared to address this directly
Call-screening service — Sanobio's number blocks unknown callers; closer's number must be sent to Sanobio before 10:30AM tomorrow or the call will not connect
exact_promises_rep_made: Specialist will call at 10:30AM on April 21 for a phone appointment
Closer's phone number will be sent to Sanobio before the appointment so it is not blocked by his screening service
Text message with appointment details will be sent after the call
closer_should_know: Sanobio's number has a call-screening service — if the closer's number is not sent to him before 10:30AM tomorrow, the call will not connect and the sit is lost. Beyond logistics: Sanobio booked with 'not right now' intact and pricing anxiety unresolved. The closer's first job is to re-establish why the conversation is worth his time, then address the per-kWh rate question directly and early. Do not lead with credit qualification — he refused it explicitly and it will trigger an exit.
